name: Andorra (Principality of Andorra / Principat d'Andorra)
summary: Andorra is a Western European civil-law constitutional co-principality (uniquely co-headed by the Bishop of Urgell in Spain and the President of France as Co-Princes) whose family-law framework operates under the Civil Code (Codi de la Família) and Qualified Law on Marriage 2017 (Llei Qualificada del Matrimoni). Parental authority (potestat parental) and child custody are governed by Civil Code arts. 27-58 and Law 14/2019 on Children's and Adolescents' Rights. The Superior Court of Justice (Tribunal Superior de Justícia) is the apex court for civil and criminal matters; the Constitutional Court (Tribunal Constitucional) operates separate constitutional review. Family-law matters are heard at first instance in the Court of Magistrates (Tribunal de Batlles). Psychology profession is regulated through the Andorran Order of Psychologists. Andorra is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label; courts operate substantively under the superior-interest-of-the-child standard codified in Law 14/2019. Andorra acceded to the Hague Convention 1980 effective 1 July 2011. Andorra is a Council of Europe member subject to ECHR jurisdiction.

statutory_framework.citation: Civil Code (Codi de la Família) arts. 27-58
statutory_framework.title: Civil Code — Parental authority
statutory_framework.relevance: Federal Civil Code Family Code drawing on Catalan civil-law substantive heritage. Arts. 27-58 govern potestat parental and child custody.

statutory_framework.citation: Law 14/2019 on Children's and Adolescents' Rights
statutory_framework.title: Law on Children's and Adolescents' Rights
statutory_framework.relevance: Federal Law on children's rights aligned with UNCRC obligations.

apex_courts.name: Superior Court of Justice (Tribunal Superior de Justícia)
apex_courts.role: Apex court for civil and criminal matters.

apex_courts.name: Constitutional Court (Tribunal Constitucional)
apex_courts.role: Constitutional Court with original jurisdiction over constitutional review.

professional_regulators.name: Andorran Order of Psychologists (COPAND)
professional_regulators.role: Federal professional order for psychologists in Andorra.

anonymisation_convention: Andorran family-court decisions are anonymised per Superior Court practice using initials.

key_developments.title: Modern Constitution + Council of Europe membership prep
key_developments.description: Modern Constitution adopted establishing democratic framework; preparations for Council of Europe membership.

key_developments.title: Council of Europe membership
key_developments.description: Andorra joined the Council of Europe; ECHR became applicable.

key_developments.title: Hague Convention 1980 accession
key_developments.description: Andorra acceded to the Hague Convention 1980 effective 1 July 2011.

key_developments.title: Law 14/2019 on Children's and Adolescents' Rights
key_developments.description: Federal Law on children's rights enacted aligned with UNCRC obligations.

structural_findings: Andorra operates a Catalan-civil-law family-law framework — places Andorra in the Catalan civil-law tradition cluster.
Co-principality constitutional structure (Bishop of Urgell + President of France as Co-Princes) is structurally distinctive globally — only co-principality in the corpus.
Council of Europe + ECHR + Hague Convention places Andorra in the European civil-law cluster.
Catalan official-language framework is structurally distinctive within Europe in the corpus.
editorial_notes: Andorra jurisdiction sidecar — civil-law Western European co-principality micro-state (Civil Code Catalan + Law 14/2019 Children's Rights + ECHR + Hague Convention 1980 accession 2011).
PA-recognition: silent statutory + no-apex-position + silent regulator.
Joins Western European + civil-law + Catalan + ECHR + Hague Convention + co-principality-globally-distinctive + micro-state clusters within the corpus.
